……今日は妙に体格のいいやつが多いな。<br>モンスターが出たなんて報告はなかっ:
...There sure are a lot of well-built guys today. No reports of
monsters, but...
別に殺気立ってる様子もないし、<br>心配ないとは思うけど……ん？:
They don't seem hostile, so I think there's nothing to worry about...
Huh?
ほら、ご飯だよ～。<br>たくさん食べてね♪: Here, it's mealtime! Eat plenty, okay?♪
あれはつい最近、前線基地に来た……たしか、ヒュメナだったな。<br>せっかくだ、:
That's the one who came to the Frontline Base recently... It's Humena,
right? Since I'm here, maybe I'll chat with her.
ああ。ちゃんと話すのは初めてだな。<br>ここの生活には慣れたか？ 困っているこ:
Yeah. This is the first time we've really talked. Getting used to life
here? Let me know if anything's bothering you.
ふふ、ありがと。<br>でもおかげさまで、上手くやれてるよ。: Fufu, thanks. But thanks to you, I'm doing fine.
基地のみんなは親切だし、<br>一緒に戦う仲間だからね♪: Everyone at the base is kind, and we're comrades fighting together!♪
そうか、ならいいんだ。<br>ところで何してたんだ？ 餌やりか？: I see, that's good. So what were you up to? Feeding her?
ほらウパちゃん、司令官君に挨拶して～。: Come on, Upa-chan, say hi to the Commander!
うぉっ。そいつ、魚なのに鳴くのかっ？: Whoa! That thing's a fish, but it makes noise?
うん！ ウパって鳴くからウパちゃんって呼んでるんだ～。<br>可愛いでしょ♪:
Yeah! Because it goes "Upa! Upa!", I call her Upa-chan! Isn't she
cute?♪
あ、ああ……なんだか妙に綺麗だしな。<br>どういう生き物なんだ？: Ah, yeah... She's oddly beautiful. What kind of creature is she?
ウパちゃんはね、ただのお魚じゃないんだよ！<br>水の精霊で、水神を祀る神官の私:
Upa-chan isn't just an ordinary fish! She's a water spirit who serves
me, a priestess of the Water God!
だから私もお礼に教会の秘術で、ウパちゃんが住みやすい水槽を<br>作ってるんだ～:
So in return, I use the church's secret arts to make a comfortable
tank for Upa-chan! Oh, this is that tank!
この水槽って見た目は小さいけど、実は中はとっても広くて――<br>あっ、違う違う:
This tank might look small on the outside, but it's actually really
spacious inside—Oh, no, no, that's not it! I've got to introduce Upachan now!
えとえと、とにかくウパちゃんは私と大の仲良しで<br>いつも一緒にいるんだけど、:
Um, um, anyway, Upachan and I are really close, and we're always
together, and she's just so, so cute!
まずは何と言っても、このプニプニのほっぺたとつぶらな瞳！<br>愛らしくてたまら:
First of all, these squishy cheeks and round eyes! She's so adorable I
can't stand it, right?
（マ、マシンガントークすぎる！！<br>相槌を打つ余地すらないぞ！？）: (She's talking way too fast! I can't even get a word in!)
（いやまあ、好きなものについて聞かれると饒舌になるタイプなんだろう……<br>こ:
(Well, I suppose she's the type to get chatty when asked about what
she likes... Guess I'll just listen until she's happy.)
もちろん可愛いだけじゃないんだよ！ きらきらの鱗とすべすべの肌は<br>綺麗だし:
Of course she's not just cute! Her shiny scales and smooth skin are
beautiful, and her fins and gills look strong and cool—!
いや、そのぉ……私１人で話しすぎちゃってるなーって……あはは！:
Well, um... I've been talking way too much by myself, haven't I...
Ahaha!
いや、全く構わないぞ？<br>そういう話が聞きたくて声をかけたんだ。:
No, I don't mind at all. I came over because I wanted to hear about
this kind of thing.
ヒュメナのことを知るなら、<br>好きなものについて聞くのが一番だろう。:
If I want to get to know you, asking about the things you love is the
best way, isn't it?
司令官君……。<br>うん！ いい子だね、君！: Commander... Yeah, you're a good kid!
なんだ、さっきまで子どものように語っていたくせに、<br>急にこっちを子ども扱い:
What's this? You were talking like a child yourself just now, and now
you're treating me like one?
もー！ それは言わないでってばー！: Geez, I told you not to say that!
おーっと！<br>ここで最高記録、３７枚だー！: Whoa there, that's a new record: 37 tiles!
…………？ なんだか盛り上がってるみたいだね。<br>あっちは訓練所だったっけ？:
...? Looks like things are getting lively over there. That's the training
grounds, isn't it? I wonder what's going on?
ねね。ちょっと行ってみようよ、司令官君っ。: Hey, hey. Let's go check it out, Commander!
さあさあ、次の挑戦者はいないか！？: Come on, now! Anyone else want to challenge?
あ、司令官殿！<br>実は、誰が瓦を一番多く割れるかを競う、大会を開いているんで:
Ah, Lord Commander! Actually, we're holding a tournament to see who
can break the most tiles!
瓦割り――というホウライの伝統的な力試しで、<br>たくさんの兵士や力自慢が集ま:
It's a traditional Hourai test of strength—tile breaking. Plenty of
soldiers and strongmen have gathered for it!
なるほど……。<br>体格のいい男が集まっていると思ったら、そういうことだったの: I see... So that's why all the burly men were gathered around.
へー、面白そうだね。<br>司令官君も挑戦してみたら？: Huh, sounds fun. Why don't you give it a try too, Commander?
俺は頭脳派なんでな。<br>こんなの１枚も割れずに怪我をするだけさ。:
I'm more of a brainy type. I'd just get hurt without breaking a single
one.
ふーん……。<br>じゃあ私が代わりに参加してみよっかな？: Hmm... Well then, maybe I'll take part instead?
ヒュメナが……？<br>神官なんだろう？ 大丈夫なのか？:
Humena? You're a priestess, aren't you? Are you sure that's a good
idea?
もっちろん。水の精霊には力が強い子もいて、<br>よく鍛錬に付き合ってもらってる:
Of course! Some water spirits are incredibly strong, and I always
have them help me train!
……その水の精霊に腕力強いイメージがないんだが。: I never really pictured water spirits as being that strong...
あ、信じてないなー？<br>よーし、水の精霊パワーを見せてあげましょう！:
Oh, you don't believe me? All right then, I'll show you the power of a
water spirit!
おいおい、大丈夫なのか姉ちゃん？<br>こいつは素人が割るにはなかなか難しいんだ:
Hey, hey, you sure about this, lady? These tiles aren't easy for a
beginner to break.
俺の最高記録、３７枚を超えられるかな？<br>ま、無理だろうけどな！ はっはっは:
Think you can beat my record of thirty-seven? Nah, I doubt it!
Hahaha!
まあ、何はともあれ怪我はしないでくれよ？: Well, just make sure you don't hurt yourself, all right?
任せておいて。<br>かっこいいところ見せちゃうからねっ！: Leave it to me! I'll show you something awesome!
ヒュメナが積み上げられた瓦の前で、<br>ゆっくりと腕を振り上げる。そして――:
Humena stood before the stacked tiles and slowly raised her arm. And
then...
瓦を割ったとは思えない爆音が響く！<br>衝撃が収まると、そこには――:
An explosion of sound, nothing like tile breaking, echoed through the
area! When the shock subsided, what lay there was...
嘘だろ！？ １００枚積んであったんだぞ！？: No way! There were a hundred of them stacked!
（……確かに力も素晴らしい。だが、それだけじゃない。<br>テクニック、スピード:
(Her strength's remarkable, but it's not just that. Technique, speed,
determination. Spirit, technique, body—that's what does it.)
司令官君、見てた見てた～？: Commander, you saw that, didn't you?
ああ、正直ここまでとは思わなかったよ。: Yeah, I honestly didn't expect you to go that far.
ふっふっふー。<br>これが水の精霊パワーだよ！！: Fufufu! That's the power of a water spirit!
（こりゃ、頼れる戦力が来てくれたな……）: (Well, that's a reliable ally to have...)
